examples.title: Algorithm: CRC Generation
examples.description: Cyclic Redundancy Check calculation for error detection
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateCRC
BEGIN
 INPUT: data (binary string), generator (polynomial)
 
 // Append zeros equal to degree of generator
 padded_data = data + '0'*(generator_length-1)
 
 // Convert to polynomial representation
 dividend = padded_data
 divisor = generator
 
 // Perform polynomial division
 FOR i FROM 0 TO LENGTH(data)-1 DO
 IF dividend[i] == '1' THEN
 dividend[i:i+generator_length] = XOR(dividend[i:i+generator_length], divisor)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Return final remainder as CRC
 crc = dividend[-generator_length+1:]
 OUTPUT crc
END
examples.explanation:
1. Appends n-1 zeros to data for division
2. Performs bitwise XOR division with generator polynomial
3. Remainder becomes CRC value appended to original data

examples.title: Algorithm: Checksum Calculation
examples.description: Internet checksum for error detection in network packets
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateChecksum
BEGIN
 INPUT: data (array of 16-bit words)
 
 sum = 0
 
 // Sum all 16-bit words
 FOR EACH word IN data DO
 sum = sum + word
 // Handle carry overflow
 IF sum > 0xFFFF THEN
 sum = (sum & 0xFFFF) + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Return one's complement
 checksum = ~sum & 0xFFFF
 OUTPUT checksum
END
examples.explanation:
1. Sum all data words with carry wrap-around
2. Take one's complement of final sum
3. Used in IP, TCP, and UDP headers

examples.title: Algorithm: Stop-and-Wait ARQ
examples.code:
ALGORITHM StopAndWaitARQ
BEGIN
 // Sender Side
 WHILE has_more_frames DO
 send_frame(current_seq)
 start_timer()
 
 WAIT UNTIL (ACK received OR timeout)
 
 IF timeout THEN
 resend_frame(current_seq)
 ELSE IF ACK.seq == current_seq THEN
 current_seq = 1 - current_seq
 move_to_next_frame()
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 // Receiver Side
 WHILE TRUE DO
 WAIT FOR frame
 
 IF frame.seq == expected_seq THEN
 deliver_data()
 send_ack(expected_seq)
 expected_seq = 1 - expected_seq
 ELSE
 send_ack(1 - expected_seq)
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation:
1. Sender waits for ACK before next transmission
2. Uses sequence numbers (0/1) for frame identification
3. Implements timeout and retransmission mechanism
